retry_reason: The function always succeeds in returning a valid label with an acquired reference; no failure path exists, so retry is never needed.
contract_summary: This function inserts a label into a label set or retrieves an existing equivalent label. It always returns a pointer to an aa_label that holds a newly acquired reference (via aa_get_label or __aa_get_label). The caller is responsible for calling aa_put_label on the returned label when it is no longer needed. If a duplicate is found and is alive, the function returns the existing label with an additional reference; if the duplicate is stale or replace is requested, the new label replaces the old one and a reference to the new label is returned. The tree insertion and proxy sharing are internal details that guarantee the returned label is properly integrated into the label set's lifecycle.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to obtain a reference-counted label from a label set, either by finding an existing one or inserting a new one. The tree operations and proxy sharing are part of the get-or-insert mechanics, but the core contract is acquiring a reference that the caller must later release.

check.reason: Both operations correctly identify that the function returns a label pointer with an incremented reference count. Candidate 1 corresponds to the __aa_get_label(this) path when an existing non-stale label is found and returned; the access path this.count.refcount and type chain aa_label--kref--refcount_t match the callee's (__aa_get_label) path and chain.
